home.tagline: Learn to code by visually <hl>reverse-engineering</hl> components.
home.subtitle: Look at the target. Rebuild it. The gauge tells you how close you are.
home.aiSubtitle: Gemini crafts a custom challenge for you

generate.themePlaceholder: e.g. weather card, notification badge, loader...

manual.title: The Manual
manual.intro: Every CSS building block you need for the levels. Click to expand.

displayFlex.summary: Turns on flexbox mode. All children align in a row (by default) with powerful alignment tools.

flexDirection.summary: Direction of flex children. `row` (default) = horizontal. `column` = vertical.

justifyContent.summary: Aligns children on the main axis (horizontal in row, vertical in column).
justifyContent.values: flex-start · center · flex-end · space-between · space-around · space-evenly

alignItems.summary: Aligns children on the cross axis. Combined with `justify-content: center`, centers everything.

gap.summary: Space between flex/grid children. No more margin tricks.

displayGrid.summary: 2D grid mode. For strict rows/columns layouts.

padding.summary: INSIDE space of the element. Pushes content toward the center.

margin.summary: OUTSIDE space. Pushes neighbors away. Trick: `margin: 0 auto` centers horizontally.

background.summary: Background color or image. Also supports gradients.

borderRadius.summary: Rounds the corners. `50%` = perfect circle (if the element is square).

border.summary: Border around the element. Format: thickness, style, color.

boxShadow.summary: Drop shadow. Format: `x y blur color`. Adds depth.

fontWeight.summary: Text weight. `400` = normal, `700` = bold, `800-900` = extra bold.

marginAuto.summary: Centers an element horizontally when its width is set. `auto` splits left/right space equally.

opacity.summary: Controls transparency (0 = invisible, 1 = opaque). `0.5` = semi-transparent. Different from `visibility: hidden` which keeps the space.

selectorClass.summary: Targets every tag with the matching `class="..."`. A class can be applied to multiple tags.

selectorId.summary: Targets ONE specific tag by its `id`. An id must be unique on the page. Higher specificity than classes.

selectorUniversal.summary: The asterisk targets ALL tags. Useful for a reset or `box-sizing`.

combinatorDescendant.summary: Descendant selector: `A B` matches every `B` inside an `A`, at any nesting level.

combinatorChild.summary: Direct child selector: `A > B` matches only `B`s that are IMMEDIATE children of `A` (not deeper).

combinatorAdjacent.summary: Adjacent sibling: `A + B` matches only the `B` directly following an `A` at the same level.

combinatorSibling.summary: General sibling: `A ~ B` matches every `B` following an `A` at the same level (not necessarily adjacent).

attrSelector.summary: Targets by attribute. `[attr]` = presence, `[attr=val]` = equal, `[attr^=val]` = starts with, `[attr$=val]` = ends with, `[attr~=val]` = contains word.

pseudoHover.summary: Style applied when the cursor is over the element. For links: `:link`, `:visited`, `:hover`, `:active`.

pseudoFirstChild.summary: Targets the FIRST child tag of a parent. Variants: `:last-child`, `:first-of-type`.

pseudoNthChild.summary: Targets the Nth child tag. Accepts a number, keyword (`odd`, `even`) or formula (`2n+1`, `3n`).

pseudoFirstLetter.summary: Pseudo-ELEMENT (`::`): targets the first letter of the tag's content. Useful for drop caps.

pseudoBefore.summary: Pseudo-elements that INJECT content before/after. `content` is mandatory, even empty (`content: ""`).

specificity.summary: When two rules conflict, the highest specificity wins. Score: id (100) > class (10) > tag (1). Inline (`style="..."`) beats everything except `!important`.

positionStatic.summary: Default position. The element follows the document flow. Ignores `top`, `left`, `right`, `bottom`, `z-index`.

positionRelative.summary: Stays in the flow but can be offset via `top`/`left`/`right`/`bottom`. Often used as a REFERENCE POINT for an `absolute` child.

positionAbsolute.summary: Out of normal flow. Positioned relative to the nearest positioned parent (relative/absolute/fixed). No positioned parent → relative to `<html>`.

positionFixed.summary: Out of flow. Position relative to the VIEWPORT — stays visible while scrolling. Used for fixed headers/menus.

zIndex.summary: Controls the stacking order. Higher number = in front. Only works when `position` is not `static`.

floatLeft.summary: Floats the element left or right. Neighbors wrap around the float. Classic use: image with surrounding text.

clearBoth.summary: Prevents an element from wrapping next to a floated sibling. `clear: left | right | both` per side to skip.

displayBlock.summary: `block` (default for div/p/h1) takes full width with breaks. `inline` (span/a) flows with text. `inline-block` mixes both.
displayBlock.values: block · inline · inline-block · flex · grid · none

displayNone.summary: Removes the element from the visual DOM AND from the flow (space is freed). Unlike `visibility: hidden` which keeps the space.

visibilityHidden.summary: Hides the element BUT keeps its place in the flow. The user sees nothing there, but the layout isn't affected.

transition.summary: Animates a property change on a trigger (typically `:hover`). Format: `transition: <prop> <duration> <function>`.

transitionTiming.summary: Acceleration curve of the transition. Affects the feel of motion.
transitionTiming.values: ease (default) · linear · ease-in · ease-out · ease-in-out · cubic-bezier(...)

keyframes.summary: Continuous/cyclic animation via defined steps. `@keyframes` declares the sequence; `animation-name`, `-duration`, `-iteration-count`, `-direction` apply it.

mediaQuery.summary: Applies CSS rules under conditions (width, height, orientation, color, resolution). Operators: `and`, `not`, `only`, comma for OR.

viewport.summary: The viewport meta tells the mobile browser the size to use. WITHOUT it, mobile renders the page like a desktop screen and zooms out.

cssVar.summary: CSS variables defined with `--name: value` (often in `:root`) and used with `var(--name)`. Centralizes colors/sizes/themes.

01-center-div.title: Center a div (lol)
01-center-div.brief: The ultimate myth. Center the purple square in the middle, vertically and horizontally.

02-card.title: Profile card
02-card.brief: Recreate a card with a round avatar, bold name, and a gray subtitle.

03-button-row.title: Trio of buttons
03-button-row.brief: Three buttons side by side with even gap and distinct colors.

04-pricing-card.title: Pricing card
04-pricing-card.brief: A pricing box with title, big number, and a "Choose" button at the bottom.

05-notification-badge.title: Notification badge
05-notification-badge.brief: A bell with a small red badge in the top-right showing '3'.

06-search-bar.title: Search bar
06-search-bar.brief: An input with a magnifier icon on the left, light gray background, rounded corners.

07-tab-bar.title: Tab bar
07-tab-bar.brief: Three inline tabs, 'Home' active (indigo underline, dark text).

08-stat-card.title: Stat card
08-stat-card.brief: A card with a subtle label on top, a large value, and a green '+12 %' delta.

09-positioned-tag.title: Absolute tag on a card
09-positioned-tag.brief: A card with a "NEW" tag pinned to the top-right via position: absolute.

10-pseudo-quote.title: Pseudo-elements in action
10-pseudo-quote.brief: A quote with opening/closing quotation marks via ::before and ::after.

11-css-variables.title: Theme via CSS variables
11-css-variables.brief: Define a palette in :root and reuse it via var() — warning alert.

12-keyframes-spinner.title: Animated spinner (@keyframes)
12-keyframes-spinner.brief: A circle spinning forever via @keyframes + animation infinite linear.

aiLevel.noLevel: No AI level in session. Go back home to generate one.

quiz.title: Quiz
quiz.subtitle: Review CSS concepts in short cards. Correct answer = the card waits longer before coming back.
quiz.seedNotice: 25 starter cards. Full course coverage coming after the PDF is parsed.

css-001.prompt: Which CSS declaration turns on flexbox on an element?
css-001.explanation: `display: flex` makes the container a flex container; its children become flex items.

css-002.prompt: Which property aligns flex children on the cross axis?
css-002.explanation: `align-items` acts on the axis perpendicular to `flex-direction`.

css-003.prompt: Which property aligns flex children on the main axis?
css-003.explanation: `justify-content` acts on the main axis (horizontal in `row`, vertical in `column`).

css-004.prompt: Fill in: to stack flex children vertically, write `flex-direction: ___;`
css-004.explanation: `column` flips the main axis to vertical.

css-005.prompt: Which property sets the space BETWEEN flex or grid children?
css-005.explanation: `gap` replaces margin hacks for flex and grid containers.

css-006.prompt: Which property creates INSIDE space (between content and border)?
css-006.explanation: `padding` is inside. `margin` pushes neighbors, outside the border.

css-007.prompt: Which property pushes neighbors away (OUTSIDE space)?
css-007.explanation: `margin` is outside the border; `padding` is inside.

css-008.prompt: How do you target the class `card`?
css-008.explanation: Prefix `.` for a class, `#` for an id, no prefix for a tag.

css-009.prompt: How do you target the element with `id="menu"`?
css-009.explanation: Prefix `#` for an id. An id should appear only once per page.

css-010.prompt: How do you style a button on HOVER?
css-010.explanation: Pseudo-class `:hover` with a SINGLE `:`. Pseudo-elements (like `::before`) use `::`.

css-011.prompt: Which of these rules has the HIGHEST specificity?
css-011.explanation: Specificity: id (100) > class (10) > tag (1). `#box` beats everything else.

css-012.prompt: Which unit is relative to the root (`<html>`) font size?
css-012.explanation: `rem` = root em. `em` is relative to the parent element.

css-013.prompt: Fill in: 100% of the viewport height is `100___`

css-014.prompt: Which of these formats represents the color BLUE?
css-014.explanation: `#0000ff` is the hex code for pure blue. Hex reads as `#RRGGBB`.

css-015.prompt: Which syntax makes a color SEMI-transparent?
css-015.explanation: `rgba(r, g, b, alpha)` or the modern `rgb(r g b / alpha)`.

css-016.prompt: Which positioning removes the element from the flow AND places it relative to the nearest positioned parent?
css-016.explanation: `absolute` positions relative to the nearest ancestor with `position: relative|absolute|fixed|sticky`.

css-017.prompt: Which positioning pins the element to the VIEWPORT (not the scroll)?
css-017.explanation: `fixed` sticks to the viewport. `sticky` sticks after a scroll threshold.

css-018.prompt: Fill in: to turn a square into a perfect circle, `border-radius: ___;`
css-018.explanation: `50%` rounds to half the width. On a square, it produces a circle.

css-019.prompt: Which `box-shadow` gives a soft drop shadow downward?
css-019.explanation: Format: `offset-x offset-y blur color`. Positive Y pushes the shadow down.

css-020.prompt: Which declaration corresponds to a NORMAL-weight text?
css-020.explanation: `400` = normal. `700` = bold. Numbers are preferred over keywords.

css-021.prompt: Which property centers text HORIZONTALLY inside its container?
css-021.explanation: `text-align: center` acts on text; `justify-content: center` acts on flex children.

css-022.prompt: What is the DEFAULT `display` value for a `<div>`?

css-023.prompt: How do you HIDE an element AND remove its space in the flow?
css-023.explanation: `display: none` removes the element. `visibility: hidden` hides it but keeps its space.

css-024.prompt: Which syntax applies CSS only on screens under 768px?

css-025.prompt: Which declaration animates all properties over 1 second on state change?
css-025.explanation: `transition: all 1s` covers every animatable property. Prefer targeting a specific property for perf.

css-058.prompt: How do you link an external stylesheet?

css-059.prompt: Which CSS application method is the LEAST recommended for maintenance?
css-059.explanation: Inline (`style="..."`) — you'd have to edit every tag in every document.

css-060.prompt: A CSS rule is made of which two parts?
css-060.explanation: `selector { declaration }`. The declaration contains properties and their values.

css-061.prompt: Which is the correct CSS comment syntax?
css-061.explanation: Always `/* ... */`. JS `//` does NOT work in CSS.

css-062.prompt: Which operator selects ALL tags in the document?
css-062.explanation: The asterisk `*` is the universal selector.

css-063.explanation: Descendant combinator: `p` inside a `td` inside a `table`, at any nesting level.

css-064.prompt:
Which selector targets ONLY direct child ``s of `

` (not deeper)?
css-064.explanation: The `>` operator means direct parent-child, not any descendant.

css-065.explanation: Adjacent sibling: only the `p` IMMEDIATELY after an `h1`.

css-066.explanation: General sibling: every `p` following an `h1` at the same level, not necessarily adjacent.

css-068.prompt: Which syntax targets images whose alt STARTS with "Figura"?
css-068.explanation: `^=` = starts with. `$=` = ends with. `~=` = contains the word.

css-069.prompt: Which syntax targets images whose alt ENDS with ".png"?

css-070.prompt: How do you target paragraphs WITHOUT a `lang` attribute?

css-071.prompt:
Which syntax targets the FIRST `

` child of a parent?
css-071.explanation: Pseudo-class `:first-child` with ONE colon. Targets a DOM position.

css-072.prompt:
How do you target the 5th `

` child?
css-072.explanation: `:nth-child(N)` accepts an index, keyword (`odd`, `even`) or a formula (`2n+1`).

css-073.prompt: Which syntax targets the FIRST LETTER of a paragraph?
css-073.explanation: Pseudo-ELEMENT with DOUBLE colons: `::first-letter`. Targets a PART of the content, not the box.

css-074.prompt: What's the modern convention for pseudo-elements (vs pseudo-classes)?

css-075.prompt: Which declaration centers an element HORIZONTALLY via margin auto?
css-075.explanation: `margin: 0 auto` splits left/right space evenly, centering the element.

css-076.prompt: Which property caps an element's MAX width?

css-077.explanation: The order `width style color` is flexible. CSS knows each value by its kind.

css-078.explanation: `opacity` goes from `0` (invisible) to `1` (opaque). No percentage form.

css-079.prompt: Which element appears ON TOP of others in the stack?
css-079.explanation: Higher `z-index` = higher in stack. Only works when `position` is not `static`.

css-080.explanation: Without a non-static position, `z-index` is ignored. You need `relative`, `absolute`, `fixed`, or `sticky`.

css-081.prompt: Which is NOT a valid `float` value?

css-082.prompt: Which property prevents an element from wrapping around a float?
css-082.explanation: `clear: left | right | both` breaks the wrap behavior.

css-083.prompt: Which is NOT a valid CSS media type?
css-083.explanation: Valid types: `all`, `screen`, `print`, `braille`, `handheld`, `tv`. Not `mobile` — use media queries.

css-084.prompt: Which operator is NOT valid in a media query?
css-084.explanation: Media queries accept `and`, `not`, `only`. For OR, use a comma (`,`).

css-085.prompt: How do you target portrait screens?

css-086.prompt: Which HTML tag declares the viewport for mobile responsive?
css-086.explanation: Meta tag `<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">` in `<head>`.

css-087.prompt: Which property controls the ACCELERATION CURVE of a transition?

css-088.prompt: Which syntax declares a keyframe animation?

css-089.prompt: Which keywords represent the START and END of a keyframe animation?

css-090.prompt: How do you use a CSS variable declared in `:root`?
css-090.explanation: `var(--variable-name)`. Variables are declared with `--name: value;` often in `:root`.

css-091.prompt: What's the semantic difference between `<span>` and `<div>`?
css-091.explanation: `<div>` = block (line break before/after). `<span>` = inline (flows with text).

js-001.prompt: Which keyword declares a constant (immutable value) in JS?

js-002.prompt: Which statement about var/let/const is FALSE?
js-002.explanation: They differ: `var` has function scope, `let` and `const` have block scope.

js-003.prompt: Which is NOT a JS primitive type?
js-003.explanation: Primitives: String, Number, Boolean, null, undefined, Symbol, BigInt. No separate `Integer` — all numbers are Number.

js-004.explanation: Arrays are objects in JS. `typeof []` returns `"object"`. Use `Array.isArray()` to discriminate.

js-005.explanation: Historical JS bug: `typeof null` returns `"object"` even though `null` is NOT an object.

js-006.explanation: `==` does type coercion. `5 == "5"` is `true` because "5" is converted to 5.

js-007.explanation: `===` does NOT coerce. Types differ (number vs string), so `false`.

js-008.explanation: Precedence: `&&` before `||`. So `(true && false) || true` = `false || true` = `true`.

js-009.prompt: Fill in: increment a variable by 1 is `i ___`

js-010.prompt: Which syntax uses a template literal for concatenation?
js-010.explanation: Backticks `` ` `` with `${expression}`. More readable than classic `+`.

js-011.prompt: What's the syntax of a ternary operation?

js-012.prompt: Which keyword prevents switch cases from cascading?
js-012.explanation: `break;` exits the switch. Without break, execution falls through.

js-013.prompt: What's the correct classic `for` loop syntax?
js-013.explanation: `for (init; condition; increment)`. Three parts separated by `;`.

js-014.prompt: Which loop is guaranteed to execute its block AT LEAST ONCE?
js-014.explanation: `do { ... } while (cond)` tests AFTER the body, so at least one iteration.

js-015.prompt: Which loop iterates over an object's KEYS?
js-015.explanation: `for (let prop in obj)` gives property names. `for...of` doesn't work on plain objects (without helpers).

js-016.explanation: `forEach((item, index, array) => ...)`. Item first, then index.

js-017.prompt: What's the correct named function syntax in JS?
js-017.explanation: `function name(args) { ... }`. Other syntaxes (`def`, `func`) are from other languages.

js-018.prompt: What's the correct arrow function syntax?
js-018.explanation: `(args) => expression` or `(args) => { block }`. The `=>` is mandatory.

js-021.prompt: How do you access the `nom` property of an object `p`?

js-022.prompt: How do you remove a property from an object?
js-022.explanation: `delete p.nom` removes the property. `p.nom = null` keeps it but with null value.

js-023.prompt: What does `const { cidade, rua } = morada;` do?
js-023.explanation: Object destructuring: creates `cidade` and `rua` variables from `morada`'s same-named properties.

js-024.prompt: How do you merge two objects `a` and `b`?
js-024.explanation: Spread operator: `const c = { ...a, ...b }`. On conflict, `b` overwrites `a`.

js-025.prompt: What does `this` refer to in an object method?
js-025.explanation: `this` is the object on which the method is called. Different in arrow functions!

js-026.prompt: Which keyword initializes a class in JS?
js-026.explanation: `constructor(args) { ... }` is the RESERVED name for the class init method.

js-027.explanation: `class Professor extends Pessoa`. Use `super(args)` to call the parent.

js-028.prompt: Which property gives an array's element count?

js-029.prompt: Which method adds an item to the END of an array?
js-029.explanation: `push()` adds at the end. `unshift()` adds at the start. Both modify the original.

js-030.prompt: Which method removes the FIRST element of an array?
js-030.explanation: `shift()` removes the first. `pop()` removes the last. `unshift()` ADDS to the start.

js-031.prompt: Which method adds an item to the START of an array?
js-031.explanation: `unshift()` adds at the start. `push()` adds at the end.

js-032.prompt: Which method creates a NEW array keeping only certain items?

js-033.prompt: Which method TRANSFORMS each item into a new one?
js-033.explanation: `map(cb)` returns a new array where each item is the result of `cb`.

js-034.prompt: Which method REDUCES an array to a single value?
js-034.explanation: `reduce((acc, item) => ..., initial)` accumulates a single result.

js-035.explanation: `split` cuts a string into an array by separator. Here: `["John", "Doe"]`.

js-036.explanation: `charAt(n)` returns the char at index `n`. Index 0 is the first character.

js-037.prompt: Which method finds the first occurrence of a substring?
js-037.explanation: `indexOf("abc")` returns the index of the first match, or -1 if not found.

js-038.explanation: Converts the whole string to lowercase. Note: doesn't modify the original (strings are immutable).

js-039.prompt: Which method asks the user to TYPE an answer?
js-039.explanation: `prompt("message")` opens an input box. Returns the typed string, or null if cancelled.

js-040.prompt: Which method asks a yes/no CONFIRMATION?

js-041.prompt: Which timer runs ONCE after a delay?
js-041.explanation: `setTimeout(callback, ms)` runs after ms milliseconds. `setInterval` repeats.

js-042.prompt: Which timer runs REPEATEDLY at intervals?
js-042.explanation: `setInterval(callback, ms)` repeats every ms. Use `clearInterval` to stop.

js-043.prompt: Which method generates a random number between 0 and 1?
js-043.explanation: `Math.random()`. For an integer between 0 and N: `Math.floor(Math.random() * N)`.

js-046.explanation: 0 to 11 (0 = January, 11 = December). Classic bug: forgetting to add 1 for human display.

js-048.prompt: How do you select an element by its ID?
js-048.explanation: `document.getElementById("teste")` (no `#`). Fast method, returns an Element or null.

js-049.explanation: `querySelector` returns the FIRST match (or null). `querySelectorAll` returns a NodeList of all.

js-050.explanation: `innerHTML` reads/writes HTML markup. `innerText` reads/writes only the text without tags.

js-051.prompt: How do you set `background-color` via `.style` in JS?
js-051.explanation: CSS properties with `-` become camelCase in JS. `background-color` → `backgroundColor`. Bracket notation accepts the original.

js-052.prompt:
How do you create a new `

` element in JS?
js-052.explanation: `document.createElement("p")` creates an unattached element. Use `appendChild` to insert it.

js-053.prompt: Which event fires when the pointer enters an element?

js-054.prompt: Which event fires on form submission?
js-054.explanation: `onsubmit` on the `<form>`. Ideal for validation. Return `false` to cancel.

js-055.prompt: How do you prevent the default behavior of an event?
js-055.explanation: `event.preventDefault()`. Example: prevent form submission or link navigation.

js-056.prompt: In an inline handler, how do you cancel the default action?
js-056.explanation: `return false` from the handler cancels the action. Equivalent to `event.preventDefault()` + `event.stopPropagation()`.

js-057.prompt: Which property gives access to document cookies?
js-057.explanation: `document.cookie` is a string (manual parsing). Note GDPR constraints for cookie usage.
